goal:
Build a What-If Planner and Historical Replay Benchmark for the Weekly Commit Module. The What-If Planner lets users simulate hypothetical commit mutations (add/remove/modify) against a draft plan and see the impact on capacity, RCDO coverage, and risk — with an optional LLM narrative. All computation uses the existing rules engine and feature tables; the LLM only narrates. The Historical Replay Benchmark replays risk predictions against historical seed data (weeks 1-8), compares to actual reconcile outcomes (weeks 9-12), computes precision/recall/F1, and publishes results as a markdown doc.

Success criteria:
- POST /api/ai/what-if endpoint accepts hypothetical mutations and returns structured impact analysis + optional LLM narrative
- Frontend What-If panel on MyWeek page shows before/after capacity, RCDO coverage delta, risk changes, and AI narrative
- Historical replay benchmark runs via ./gradlew evalTest and produces docs/eval-results.md
- All existing tests continue to pass (npm test, npm run lint, npm run typecheck)

Constraints:
- Java 21 backend, TypeScript strict frontend, SQL only — no Python or new infrastructure
- Reuse existing PostgreSQL, Pinecone, OpenRouter, RiskDetectionService rules, read models, AiProviderRegistry
- Do NOT modify existing entity classes, migrations, or domain enums — add new DTOs/services only
- Do NOT restructure existing frontend routes/pages — integrate via composition
- Follow existing patterns: DTOs as Java records, @Service annotation, controllers in module packages, frontend components in frontend/src/components/ai/

steps.title: What-If backend DTOs and pure-computation service
steps.description:
Create the What-If Planner backend: request/response DTOs and the core WhatIfService that simulates plan mutations using existing rules and read models. No LLM calls in this step — pure computation only.

Scope: Backend only. Do NOT modify any files in frontend/src/. Do NOT modify existing services (RiskDetectionService, WeeklyPlanService, etc.) — read from them, don't change them.

Deliverables:

1. `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/dto/WhatIfRequest.java` — Java record with planId (UUID, @NotNull), userId (UUID, @NotNull), and hypotheticalChanges (List<WhatIfMutation>). WhatIfMutation is a nested record or sibling file with: action field using a nested enum `WhatIfAction` (values: ADD_COMMIT, REMOVE_COMMIT, MODIFY_COMMIT) defined alongside the record (nested inside WhatIfRequest or in the same file), commitId (nullable UUID), title (String), chessPiece (String — not the domain ChessPiece enum, to avoid coupling), estimatePoints (Integer), rcdoNodeId (nullable UUID). Use @Valid/@NotNull consistent with existing DTO records like CommitDraftAssistRequest.

2. `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/dto/WhatIfResponse.java` — Java record with: available (boolean), currentState (nested PlanSnapshot record: totalPoints int, capacityBudget int, riskSignals List<String>, rcdoCoverage Map<UUID,Integer>), projectedState (same PlanSnapshot type), capacityDelta (int), rcdoCoverageChanges (List<RcdoCoverageChange> with rcdoNodeId, rcdoTitle nullable, beforePoints, afterPoints), riskDelta (nested record: newRisks List<String>, resolvedRisks List<String>), narrative (String, null this step), recommendation (String, null this step). Add `public static WhatIfResponse unavailable()` factory method following CommitDraftAssistResponse.unavailable() pattern.

3. `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/service/WhatIfService.java` — @Service class. Inject WeeklyPlanRepository, WeeklyCommitRepository, ScopeChangeEventRepository, WorkItemRepository, WorkItemStatusHistoryRepository (do NOT inject RcdoWeekRollupRepository — RCDO coverage is computed from commits' rcdoNodeId fields, not from the rollup table). Method simulate(WhatIfRequest): WhatIfResponse that: (a) loads plan + commits from DB, (b) builds currentState snapshot (total points, budget, per-RCDO points, risk signals computed by re-implementing the 5 rules logic inline — do NOT call RiskDetectionService.detectAndStoreRiskSignals which persists), (c) clones commit list in-memory, applies mutations (ADD inserts synthetic, REMOVE filters out, MODIFY updates), (d) builds projectedState from mutated list, (e) computes deltas. Returns WhatIfResponse with narrative=null. Replicate OVERCOMMIT/UNDERCOMMIT/REPEATED_CARRY_FORWARD/BLOCKED_CRITICAL/SCOPE_VOLATILITY rule thresholds from RiskDetectionService (duplicate as package-private constants). Throw ResourceNotFoundException if plan not found.

4. `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/service/WhatIfServiceTest.java` — @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) unit test matching RiskDetectionServiceTest pattern. Mock all repositories. Test cases: (a) ADD_COMMIT pushes over budget → OVERCOMMIT in projectedState.riskSignals, (b) REMOVE_COMMIT brings under budget → resolves OVERCOMMIT, riskDelta shows resolved, (c) MODIFY_COMMIT changes rcdoNodeId → rcdoCoverageChanges shows point shift, (d) empty mutations → currentState equals projectedState, (e) plan not found → ResourceNotFoundException.

steps.title: What-If LLM narration and REST API endpoint
steps.description:
Wire WhatIfService to the LLM for narrative generation and expose the REST endpoint. The LLM receives the structured impact analysis and produces a 2-3 sentence explanation — it does NOT compute anything. Graceful degradation: when AI is unavailable, return the full structured response with narrative=null and recommendation=null.

Scope: Backend only. Do NOT modify any files in frontend/src/.

Deliverables:

1. `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/what-if.txt` — Prompt template receiving WhatIfResponse data (current state, projected state, deltas) as structured JSON in additionalContext. Output: JSON {"narrative": "2-3 sentence impact explanation", "recommendation": "optional short recommendation"}. Include 2 few-shot examples and an ## Input Data Structure section. Follow style of existing risk-signal.txt and reconcile-assist.txt prompts.

2. Update `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiContext.java` — Add `public static final String TYPE_WHAT_IF = "WHAT_IF";` constant following existing pattern.

3. Update `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProvider.java` — Add `case AiContext.TYPE_WHAT_IF -> "what-if.txt";` to loadPromptTemplate() switch and `case AiContext.TYPE_WHAT_IF -> "what-if-v1";` to resolvePromptVersion() switch. Do NOT change any other logic.

4. Update `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/StubAiProvider.java` — Add case AiContext.TYPE_WHAT_IF in generateSuggestion() returning canned JSON {"narrative": "This change would ...", "recommendation": "Consider ..."}. Follow existing stub case patterns.

5. Update `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/service/WhatIfService.java` — After computing impact, build AiContext with suggestionType=TYPE_WHAT_IF, serialize delta data into additionalContext, call AiProviderRegistry.generateSuggestion(), parse narrative/recommendation from the JSON response string using ObjectMapper (inject ObjectMapper via constructor, following the pattern in CommitDraftAssistService and RiskDetectionService). If AI unavailable (!result.available()), return response with narrative=null, recommendation=null. Inject both AiProviderRegistry and ObjectMapper via constructor (update the constructor created in step 1 to add these two dependencies).

6. Add endpoint in `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/controller/AiController.java` — @PostMapping("/api/ai/what-if") accepting @Valid @RequestBody WhatIfRequest, calls WhatIfService.simulate(), returns ResponseEntity<WhatIfResponse>. Add WhatIfService to constructor params/fields. Follow commitDraftAssist endpoint pattern.

steps.title: What-If frontend API client and panel component
steps.description:
Build the frontend What-If panel and integrate it into the MyWeek page. The panel should always be available when the feature flag and plan state allow — it does NOT require AI to be online because the backend computation is deterministic. Only the narrative/recommendation portion degrades when AI is down.

Scope: Frontend only. Do NOT modify any files in backend/.

Deliverables:

1. `frontend/src/api/whatIfApi.ts` — Follow the createXxxApi(client, actorUserId) factory pattern used by aiApi.ts and ragApi.ts. Export createWhatIfApi(client: ApiClient, actorUserId: string) and WhatIfApi type. Define TypeScript interfaces: WhatIfMutationAction (union "ADD_COMMIT" | "REMOVE_COMMIT" | "MODIFY_COMMIT"), WhatIfMutation (action, commitId?, title?, chessPiece?, estimatePoints?, rcdoNodeId?), WhatIfRequest (planId, userId, hypotheticalChanges), PlanSnapshot (totalPoints, capacityBudget, riskSignals, rcdoCoverage), RcdoCoverageChange (rcdoNodeId, rcdoTitle?, beforePoints, afterPoints), RiskDelta (newRisks, resolvedRisks), WhatIfResponse (available, currentState, projectedState, capacityDelta, rcdoCoverageChanges, riskDelta, narrative?, recommendation?). Factory returns object with simulate: (req) => Promise<WhatIfResponse> calling client.post("/ai/what-if", req).

2. `frontend/src/api/aiHooks.ts` — Add useWhatIfApi() hook returning stable WhatIfApi instance, following the exact pattern of useAiApi(). Import createWhatIfApi from whatIfApi.ts.

3. Update `frontend/src/__tests__/MyWeekPage.test.tsx` — Add `useWhatIfApi: vi.fn(() => ({ simulate: vi.fn() }))` to the existing `vi.mock("../api/aiHooks.js", ...)` block (around line 45-60). This prevents a TypeError when WhatIfPanel calls useWhatIfApi() inside AiErrorBoundary and avoids console noise in tests. Follow the pattern of the existing useAiApi, useAiStatus, useRiskSignals mocks already in that block.

4. `frontend/src/components/ai/WhatIfPanel.tsx` — Main component. Props: planId: string, currentCommits: ReadonlyArray<CommitResponse> (from planTypes.ts). UI: collapsible card (collapsed by default, expand button "🔮 What-If Planner"). Expanded shows: (a) mutation form — action type selector (add/remove/modify), for ADD: title+chess+points inputs, for REMOVE: dropdown of current commits, for MODIFY: commit dropdown+field+value; (b) queued mutations list with remove buttons; (c) "Simulate" button calling API; (d) results section: capacity before→after with color indicator, RCDO coverage changes with +/- indicators, risk delta (new=danger, resolved=success colors), narrative in highlighted box with sparkles icon (shown only when narrative is non-null, hidden gracefully when null). Loading spinner on simulate, error on failure, graceful handling of available:false. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use useAiStatus() to hide the entire panel — the what-if computation is deterministic backend logic that works without AI. Only degrade the narrative display when narrative is null. Show the panel whenever aiAssistanceEnabled feature flag is on and the plan is in the right state. Use Card/CardHeader/CardTitle/CardContent from ../ui/Card.js, Button from ../ui/Button.js, Badge from ../ui/Badge.js. Follow CommitDraftAssistButton and ReconcileAssistPanel for styling, cn() usage, lucide-react icons.

5. Integrate into `frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx` — Import WhatIfPanel. Insert below the CommitList render and before the scope-change timeline section. Integration: {aiAssistanceEnabled && plan && (isDraft || isLocked) && (<AiErrorBoundary><WhatIfPanel planId={plan.id} currentCommits={commits} /></AiErrorBoundary>)}. AiErrorBoundary is already defined in the file. This is a minimal 3-line insertion — no restructuring of existing layout.

steps.title: What-If eval fixtures and eval runner extension
steps.description:
Add golden evaluation test cases for the What-If LLM narration capability and extend the eval runner.

Scope: Backend test resources and eval runner only. Do NOT modify any production source files.

Deliverables:

1. `backend/src/test/resources/eval/what-if/cases.json` — 8 test cases matching the EvalCase record schema (id, description, edgeCaseType, input, expectedBehavior, critical). Input contains structured what-if impact data as the LLM receives it (current state, projected state, deltas — matching WhatIfService additionalContext format). expectedBehavior has: shouldHaveNarrative (boolean), narrativeShouldMention (string array of keywords), maxNarrativeLength (int), shouldHaveRecommendation (boolean, optional). Cases: (1) add-overcommit: pushes over budget, narrative mentions overcommit, (2) remove-resolves-risk: removing resolves OVERCOMMIT, narrative mentions improvement, (3) rcdo-shift: changes RCDO coverage, narrative mentions affected branch, (4) no-impact: empty mutations, narrative indicates minimal impact, (5) mixed-mutations: add+remove, (6) budget-consumed: single large mutation consuming entire budget, (7) chess-piece-change: PAWN→KING, narrative mentions priority change, (8) undercommit-resolution: adding resolves UNDERCOMMIT.

2. Extend `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/PromptEvalRunner.java` — Add: (a) whatIfCases() method returning Stream<EvalCase> via loadCases("eval/what-if/cases.json"), (b) @ParameterizedTest(name="what-if: {0}") @MethodSource("whatIfCases") void evaluateWhatIf(EvalCase) — follows evaluateRagQuery pattern: check provider available, build AiContext with TYPE_WHAT_IF putting input into additionalContext, call provider, validate JSON has "narrative" field, create EvalResult, call evaluateWhatIfOutput(), add to allResults, assert schema valid, (c) private evaluateWhatIfOutput(JsonNode output, JsonNode expected, EvalResult) helper — checks shouldHaveNarrative (narrative present and non-empty), narrativeShouldMention (keyword matching in lowercase, same pattern as evaluateRagQueryOutput's answerShouldMention), maxNarrativeLength (string length), shouldHaveRecommendation (optional).

steps.title: Historical Replay Benchmark and eval results documentation
steps.description:
Build a test-harness that replays risk predictions against historical seed data and computes accuracy metrics, plus create the evaluation results documentation.

Scope: Backend test files and docs only. Do NOT modify any production source files or frontend. Independent of steps 1-4 for the benchmark class; the docs deliverable references step 4's what-if cases so should be written knowing that step 4 adds 8 what-if eval cases.

Deliverables:

1. `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/HistoricalReplayBenchmark.java` — @Tag("eval") @TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS) test class. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use @SpringBootTest — there are zero @SpringBootTest tests in this codebase and Testcontainers is not a dependency. Instead, follow the established pure-unit-test pattern: use plain Java objects with NO database, NO Spring context, NO external services. The benchmark creates synthetic historical data as plain Java objects (WeeklyPlan and WeeklyCommit instances built with setters, NOT persisted to any DB). Construct 10-12 weeks of synthetic plans with known outcomes using deterministic UUIDs: some RECONCILED plans that were overcommitted and had low completion, some with carry-forward streaks, some well-balanced. For each synthetic RECONCILED plan: (1) reconstruct state at lock time from the in-memory objects (planned points, commit chess pieces, carry-forward streaks via getCarryForwardStreak()), (2) run the 5 rules-based risk signal detection logic in-memory (replicate the exact thresholds from RiskDetectionService: OVERCOMMIT = totalPoints > budget, UNDERCOMMIT = totalPoints/budget < 0.60, REPEATED_CARRY_FORWARD = streak >= 2, BLOCKED_CRITICAL = King/Queen with blocked ticket for 48+ hours, SCOPE_VOLATILITY = scope changes > 3), (3) compare predictions to actual outcomes using these explicit signal-to-outcome mappings: OVERCOMMIT true positive if the plan's average commit completion rate < 70% (ACHIEVED=1.0, PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED=0.5, NOT_ACHIEVED/CANCELED=0.0); UNDERCOMMIT true positive if plan had spare capacity AND all commits ACHIEVED; REPEATED_CARRY_FORWARD true positive if the flagged commit's outcome is NOT_ACHIEVED or CANCELED; BLOCKED_CRITICAL true positive if the flagged commit's outcome is NOT_ACHIEVED; SCOPE_VOLATILITY true positive if any commit outcome is NOT_ACHIEVED, (4) aggregate per-signal-type confusion matrix → precision/recall/F1. Output results to build/eval-results/replay-benchmark.json via Jackson ObjectMapper. Since this is a pure unit test with synthetic data, setup should always succeed. Use Assumptions.assumeTrue() only as a defensive guard.

2. `docs/eval-results.md` — Markdown document: overview of eval approach (rules-based + LLM-augmented), per-capability eval summary table (commit-draft-assist: 12 cases, commit-lint: 6 cases, rcdo-suggest: 10 cases, risk-signal: 8 cases, reconcile-assist: 8 cases, rag-query: 8 cases, what-if: 8 cases — these counts match the actual files in backend/src/test/resources/eval/*/cases.json), historical replay benchmark section with metrics table (signal type / # predicted / # actual / true positives / precision / recall / F1) showing placeholder values to be filled by running ./gradlew evalTest, and "How to run" section explaining ./gradlew test (unit tests) and ./gradlew evalTest (eval tests, requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY). Note: the benchmark writes machine-readable results to build/eval-results/replay-benchmark.json; this markdown doc is the human-readable companion with placeholder tables that document the approach.
